title: Epic: Regression testing
description: Snapshot comparison, executable test export (Jest), CI-friendly output. Enables using shatter results to catch regressions.

title: JSON snapshot export and comparison
description: Implement snapshot export: serialize BehaviorClusters to a stable JSON format with function ID, behaviors, exemplar inputs/outputs, and invariants. Implement shatter diff: load a previous snapshot, re-run exploration, compare behaviors. Report matches, regressions (changed behavior), and new behaviors. Include tests with synthetic before/after snapshots.
acceptance_criteria: shatter diff snapshots/test.json correctly identifies matching behaviors, regressions, and new behaviors. Exit code is 0 for all-match, nonzero for regressions.

title: Jest test export
description: Implement shatter export-tests --framework jest: generate a Jest test file from behavior clusters. Each behavior becomes a describe/it block with the exemplar input and expected output/error. Include mock setup code for external dependencies. Generated tests should be directly runnable with npx jest.
acceptance_criteria: Generated test file for a simple function compiles and passes when run with jest. Tests cover both success and error behaviors.

title: Epic: Reporting and specification
description: Behavior clustering, Daikon-style invariant detection, markdown spec generation, and JSON snapshot export. Makes results useful to humans and agents.

title: Behavior clustering by branch path
description: Implement clustering.rs: group ExecutionRecords into BehaviorClusters based on their branch path (sequence of taken/not-taken decisions). Two executions with the same branch path belong to the same cluster. Compute cluster statistics: specimen count, input ranges, output ranges. Include tests with known execution records.
acceptance_criteria: Given 10 execution records for a 2-branch function, correctly groups them into up to 4 clusters (TT, TF, FT, FF). Cluster statistics are accurate.

title: Basic invariant detection
description: Implement invariants.rs: given a BehaviorCluster's specimens, check invariant templates against all specimens. Start with simple templates: numeric comparisons (x > 0, x == C), null checks (x \!= null), string length (s.len() > 0), output-input relations (output == input.field). Use rayon for parallel checking. Filter out trivially true invariants. Include tests with synthetic execution records.
acceptance_criteria: Given specimens where all inputs have x > 0 and output.len == input.len, detects both invariants. Does not report trivially true invariants like x is a number.

title: Markdown specification report generation
description: Implement reporter.rs: given a set of BehaviorClusters with invariants, generate the markdown specification format described in PLAN.md. Include function signature, parameter descriptions with tested ranges, one section per behavior with When/Returns/Throws/Invariant/Performance fields, edge cases section, and dependencies section. Include tests comparing generated output against expected markdown.
acceptance_criteria: Generated markdown for a known function matches the expected format. Output is parseable and readable by both humans and agents.

title: Epic: Packaging and distribution
description: Build pipeline, cross-platform binaries, installation method, frontend bundling. Users need to be able to install and run shatter easily.

title: Packaging: cross-platform Rust binary builds
description: Set up GitHub Actions CI to build shatter-cli for Linux (x86_64, aarch64), macOS (x86_64, aarch64), and Windows (x86_64). Use cross for cross-compilation. Produce static binaries. Include Z3 as a statically-linked dependency. Verify binaries run on target platforms.
acceptance_criteria: CI produces 5 platform binaries. Each binary runs shatter --help successfully on its target platform.

title: Packaging: bundle TS frontend with core
description: Bundle the TypeScript frontend so it ships with the shatter binary. Options: (1) embed the compiled JS as a resource in the Rust binary and extract at runtime, (2) ship as a companion directory alongside the binary, (3) use bun compile to produce a single-file TS frontend binary. The user should not need to run npm install separately. Include an installation test.
acceptance_criteria: A fresh install of shatter (just the binary + bundled frontend) can run shatter explore on a TypeScript file without any additional setup beyond having Node.js installed.

title: Packaging: installation script and docs
description: Create an installation script (install.sh) that downloads the correct platform binary and installs it to a standard location. Add a --version flag to the CLI. Write a README.md section covering installation, quickstart, and basic usage. Consider Homebrew formula and/or cargo install support.
acceptance_criteria: curl -sSL .../install.sh installs shatter. shatter --version prints the version. README covers installation and a 3-command quickstart.

title: Packaging: bundle Go frontend
description: Bundle the Go frontend binary alongside the shatter core binary. Cross-compile the Go frontend for the same platforms as the core. Include in the installation package.
acceptance_criteria: A fresh install of shatter can run shatter explore on Go files without separately installing the Go frontend.

title: Epic: Mocking and compositional testing
description: Automatic dependency detection, mock generation from types, scope configuration, and compositional testing (using results from testing B to mock B when testing A).

title: TS frontend: automatic dependency detection
description: Extend the TS analyzer to detect external dependencies: for each call expression in the target function, trace the symbol to its declaration. If the declaration is outside the target file/scope, report it as an ExternalDependency with symbol name, source module, return type, and call sites. Include tests with functions that import and call external modules.
acceptance_criteria: For a function that calls fetchUser from ./userService, the analyzer reports the dependency with correct symbol, module path, and return type.

title: TS frontend: mock injection via import rewriting
description: Extend the instrumentor to rewrite imports for external dependencies: replace real imports with mock registry lookups. The mock registry is populated by the core before execution based on ExternalDependency analysis. Record all calls to mocked functions (arguments, return values). Include tests demonstrating mock injection and call recording.
acceptance_criteria: A function calling an external fetchUser can be executed with a mock that returns a canned value. The call arguments are recorded and reported back to the core.

title: Scope configuration (shatter.scope.yaml)
description: Implement scope configuration parsing: read shatter.scope.yaml with include/exclude/mock/passthrough globs. The core uses this to tell frontends which dependencies to mock and which to leave as real code. Include the --scope CLI flag. Include tests for glob matching.
acceptance_criteria: Given a scope config that mocks node_modules/** and passes through lodash, the core correctly classifies dependencies. CLI accepts --scope flag.

title: Compositional testing: behavior maps
description: Implement compositional testing: when the core has already tested function B, store its behavior map (inputs -> outputs). When testing function A that calls B, instruct the frontend to mock B using the behavior map. Track which of B's behaviors A actually triggers. Implement call graph ordering (test leaf functions first). Include tests with a two-function dependency chain.
acceptance_criteria: Testing function A that calls function B uses B's recorded behavior as a mock. The core reports which behaviors of B were exercised by A.

title: Epic: TypeScript frontend (basic)
description: Get a working TypeScript frontend that can analyze functions, instrument code, execute tests, and communicate with the Rust core via the JSON protocol. Start simple: line-level instrumentation, type-aware value generation, no symbolic constraints yet.

title: Initialize TypeScript frontend project
description: Create the shatter-ts/ directory with package.json, tsconfig.json (strict mode), and a main.ts entry point. The entry point reads newline-delimited JSON protocol messages from stdin and writes responses to stdout. Implement the handshake and ping commands per the protocol spec. Validate incoming messages against the JSON schemas from the protocol task. Include jest config and a basic test. Wire up npm test and npm run build.
acceptance_criteria: npm run build produces a runnable Node.js script. Conforms to the protocol: handles handshake, responds to ping. npm test passes. Protocol conformance tests pass against the JSON schemas.

title: TS frontend: analyze function types and signatures
description: Implement analyzer.ts: given a file path and function name, use the TypeScript Compiler API to extract parameter names, types (as TypeInfo per the protocol schema), return type, and source location. Handle primitive types, arrays, objects, unions, enums, and nullable types. Respond to the analyze protocol command. Include tests with sample .ts files covering various type signatures.
acceptance_criteria: Given a TS file with function add(a: number, b: number): number, the analyzer returns correct TypeInfo for both params and return type. Tests cover primitives, objects, arrays, unions, and optional params. Response validates against the AnalyzeResponse JSON schema.

title: TS frontend: basic code instrumentation (line-level)
description: Implement instrumentor.ts: use the TS Compiler API to rewrite a target function, inserting __record(lineNumber) calls at each statement and branch point. This is the v1-style instrumentation — no symbolic constraints yet. The instrumented code should be compilable and executable. Include tests that instrument a sample function and verify the recorded lines match expectations.
acceptance_criteria: Instrumenting a simple if/else function produces code that, when run, reports which lines executed. Tests verify correct line recording for linear code, if/else, loops, and switch statements.

title: TS frontend: execute instrumented code in worker
description: Implement executor.ts: take instrumented code and a set of input values (as JSON), execute the code in a worker thread, capture the return value (or error), lines executed, and basic performance metrics (wall time). Respond to the execute protocol command. Return results conforming to the ExecuteResponse JSON schema. Include tests that execute a simple instrumented function with known inputs.
acceptance_criteria: Given instrumented code for function add(a, b) { return a + b } and inputs [2, 3], the executor returns {return_value: 5, lines_executed: [...], wall_time_ms: ...}. Error cases (thrown exceptions, timeouts) are captured correctly. Response validates against schema.

title: Create example test suite for validation
description: Create examples/ directory with TypeScript functions of increasing complexity for validating shatter capabilities: (1) pure arithmetic with branches, (2) string manipulation with conditionals, (3) object parameter with field access in conditions, (4) function with error throwing, (5) function with enum/union parameter. Each function should have known branches that serve as ground truth for testing shatter coverage.
acceptance_criteria: At least 5 example functions with comments documenting all expected branches. Can be used as integration test fixtures.

title: End-to-end: shatter explore with random inputs (no Z3)
description: Wire the Rust core to the TS frontend for end-to-end exploration. The core sends analyze to get function metadata, generates random inputs using proptest based on the TypeInfo, sends execute requests, collects ExecutionRecords, and prints an exploration log showing discovered execution paths. No symbolic solving yet — just random/type-aware input generation. This is the first externally demonstrable milestone.
acceptance_criteria: Running shatter explore examples/simple.ts:myFunc on a simple function with branches produces output showing multiple execution paths discovered, with line coverage statistics.

title: Side effect capture (console, errors, global state)
description: Extend the TS executor to capture side effects: intercept console.log/warn/error, capture thrown errors with full type/message/stack, snapshot module-level variables before/after execution. Report side effects as part of the execution record. Include tests.
acceptance_criteria: A function that calls console.log and throws an error has both side effects recorded in the execution record.

title: Performance metrics (CPU time, heap usage)
description: Extend the TS executor to report detailed performance metrics: wall time (hrtime), CPU time (cpuUsage), heap used/allocated (memoryUsage delta). Run with --expose-gc for accurate memory measurement. Include tests verifying metrics are reasonable.
acceptance_criteria: Execution records include cpu_time_us and heap_used_bytes fields with plausible values. A memory-allocating function shows measurable heap delta.

title: Epic: Rust core scaffold
description: Set up the Rust workspace, core data types, Z3 integration, frontend subprocess protocol, and CLI skeleton. Foundation for everything else.

title: Initialize Rust workspace with cargo
description: Create the Cargo workspace with two crates: shatter-core (library) and shatter-cli (binary). Set up basic dependencies: serde, serde_json, clap, thiserror. Add a placeholder main.rs that parses CLI args with clap and prints help. Add CI-ready cargo test and cargo clippy commands. Verify the binary builds and runs.
acceptance_criteria: cargo build succeeds, cargo test passes, cargo clippy -- -D warnings clean, ./target/debug/shatter --help prints usage.

title: Define SymExpr and core data types in Rust
description: Implement the core type definitions in shatter-core: SymExpr enum (Param, Const, BinOp, UnOp, Call, Unknown), ConstValue, BinOpKind, UnOpKind, TypeInfo, ParamInfo, ExecutionRecord, BranchDecision, SymConstraint. All types derive Serialize/Deserialize. Include unit tests verifying JSON round-trip for each type.
acceptance_criteria: All types defined with serde derives, unit tests pass for JSON serialization round-trip of every variant.

title: Integrate Z3 via the z3 crate
description: Add the z3 crate dependency. Implement solver.rs with: to_z3_expr() to convert SymExpr to Z3 AST nodes, solve_for_new_path() to negate a branch constraint and find a satisfying assignment, extract_concrete_values() to read values from a Z3 model. Support Int, Real, String, Bool sorts. Include unit tests with known-satisfiable and known-unsatisfiable constraint sets.
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests demonstrate solving simple constraints like x > 10 AND x < 20 finds a value, and x > 10 AND x < 5 returns unsat.

title: Define frontend protocol as JSON Schema with documentation
description: Define the JSON protocol for communication between the Rust core and language frontends. Create formal JSON Schema files (one per message type) under protocol/schemas/. Implement matching Rust types for all protocol messages: AnalyzeRequest, AnalyzeResponse, ExecuteRequest, ExecuteResponse, InstrumentRequest, InstrumentResponse, MockConfig, plus error responses. Include a protocol version field in every message. Write serde round-trip tests for every message type. Write PROTOCOL.md documenting: the wire format (newline-delimited JSON over stdio), handshake sequence, every message type with examples, error handling conventions, and versioning/compatibility rules. Include a small reference implementation of a no-op frontend that responds to all commands with stubs for use in testing and as a guide for frontend authors.
acceptance_criteria: JSON Schema files validate against example messages. All Rust message types tested for JSON round-trip. PROTOCOL.md is comprehensive with examples for every command. No-op reference frontend passes protocol conformance tests.

title: Implement frontend subprocess manager
description: Implement frontend.rs: spawn a frontend process, send JSON messages via stdin, read JSON responses from stdout. Handle process lifecycle (start, health check, shutdown). Support configurable frontend binary paths. Use tokio for async I/O. Include tests using the no-op reference frontend from the protocol task.
acceptance_criteria: Can spawn a subprocess, send a protocol message, receive a response, and shut down cleanly. Tests pass with the reference frontend.

title: Implement basic CLI with clap
description: Implement the shatter explore subcommand in shatter-cli. Parse <file>:<function> target syntax. Auto-detect language from file extension (.ts -> typescript, .go -> go). Wire up to the frontend subprocess manager (spawn the appropriate frontend). Add --max-iterations and --timeout flags. For now, just print the exploration log to stdout.
acceptance_criteria: shatter explore test.ts:myFunc spawns the TS frontend, sends an analyze request, and prints the response. shatter --help shows all subcommands.

title: Epic: Go frontend
description: Go language frontend using go/ssa, go/types, go/ast. Implements the same frontend protocol as the TS frontend.

title: Initialize Go frontend project
description: Create the shatter-go/ directory with go.mod, main.go entry point implementing the JSON protocol handler (stdin/stdout). Implement handshake and ping per the protocol spec. Validate messages against JSON schemas. Include basic tests. Wire up go test and go vet.
acceptance_criteria: go build produces a binary. Conforms to protocol: handles handshake, responds to ping. go test passes. go vet clean. Protocol conformance tests pass.

title: Go frontend: analyze function types via go/types
description: Implement Go function analysis: given a file path and function name, use go/types + go/ssa to extract parameter names, types (as TypeInfo per protocol schema), return types, and branch information. Respond to the analyze protocol command. Handle basic types (int, string, bool, float), slices, maps, structs, interfaces, and pointers. Include tests with sample Go files.
acceptance_criteria: Given a Go file with func Add(a, b int) int, the analyzer returns correct TypeInfo. Tests cover basic types, structs, slices, and interfaces. Response validates against AnalyzeResponse schema.

title: Go frontend: AST instrumentation
description: Implement Go code instrumentation using go/ast + go/printer: insert line recording and branch recording calls into if statements, switch cases, and for loops. Support both line-level recording and symbolic constraint extraction from branch conditions. The instrumented code must compile and run. Include tests.
acceptance_criteria: Instrumenting a simple Go function with if/else produces compilable code that reports which lines executed and branch decisions made.

title: Go frontend: execute instrumented code
description: Implement Go execution: compile the instrumented code into a temporary test binary, run it as a subprocess, capture JSON results via stdout. Measure wall time, CPU time, and heap allocation. Respond to the execute protocol command. Return results conforming to ExecuteResponse schema. Include tests.
acceptance_criteria: Given instrumented Go code and input values, the executor compiles, runs, and returns the execution record with return value, lines executed, and performance metrics.

title: End-to-end: shatter explore for Go functions
description: Wire the Rust core to the Go frontend for end-to-end exploration. The same shatter explore CLI command works for .go files. Include Go example functions in examples/ directory. Verify that the concolic loop (with Z3) finds branches in Go code.
acceptance_criteria: shatter explore examples/simple.go:MyFunc discovers multiple execution paths and reports coverage. Z3 solving finds non-obvious inputs for Go branch conditions.

title: Go test export
description: Implement shatter export-tests --framework gotest: generate Go test files from behavior clusters. Each behavior becomes a test function with table-driven test structure. Generated tests should be directly runnable with go test.
acceptance_criteria: Generated test file compiles and passes when run with go test. Tests cover both success and error behaviors.

title: Epic: Concolic execution for TypeScript
description: Add symbolic constraint extraction to the TS frontend and Z3-based constraint solving to the core. This is the key innovation over v1 — directed input generation instead of brute-force guessing.

title: TS frontend: symbolic constraint extraction from branches
description: Extend instrumentor.ts to emit symbolic constraints alongside line recording. For each branch condition (if, switch, ternary, &&, ||), analyze the AST to determine if the condition references function parameters. If so, emit a SymExpr representation per the protocol schema. Implement lightweight data flow tracking: when a local variable is assigned from a parameter expression, track the symbolic relationship. Fall back to Unknown for complex flows. Include tests with functions that have parameter-dependent branches.
acceptance_criteria: For function f(x: number) { if (x > 10) return big; return small; }, the frontend reports branch constraint {kind: binop, op: >, left: {kind: param, name: x}, right: {kind: const, value: 10}}. Tests cover simple comparisons, compound conditions (&&, ||), and property access (obj.field > 5).

title: Core: concolic execution loop with Z3
description: Implement the full concolic loop in orchestrator.rs: execute a function with inputs, collect branch constraints, negate each branch constraint and solve with Z3, add solutions to the worklist, repeat. Use rayon to parallelize Z3 solver calls across branches. Implement priority queue ordering (Z3-solved > fuzzed). Include integration test with a simple multi-branch function where random inputs alone cannot find all paths but Z3 can.
acceptance_criteria: Given a function f(x) { if (x === 42) return found; return not found; }, the concolic loop finds the input x = 42 via Z3 solving. Random-only exploration demonstrably fails to find it in reasonable iterations.
